summary: n8n community node executes reverse shell and internal-network recon on load
details: The package advertises itself as an n8n HTTP probing node, but its declared main entry index.js contains a self-invoking IIFE that runs the moment the package is required. On load, index.js spawns /bin/bash -i and pipes its stdio through a TCP socket to the hardcoded remote host 103.27.109.184:8893, giving that endpoint interactive shell access on the host running n8n. In the same code path it performs raw HTTP GETs against internal Docker bridge addresses (172.17.0.1, 172.17.0.2, 172.18.0.2 on ports 80/443/8080), collects the response bodies together with os.hostname(), and writes the aggregated results to the same 103.27.109.184:8893 socket. The referenced HttpProbeNode.js node stub is inert and serves as cover for the payload in index.js, which fires whenever n8n loads this community node.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
